intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Good, I have the GSM5746268 sample. Let me read the skill for standard scRNA-seq QC and clustering to ensure I follow the right procedure.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "description": "Standard scanpy processing for ONE scRNA-seq sample.", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + first-pass clustering (scanpy). Halt and present_plan before running on an unfamiliar dataset \u2014 the QC thresholds and the clustering resolution are dataset-dependent. Steps: sc.read_10x_mtx; calculate_qc_metrics; filter cells (n_genes>=200) & genes; filter pct_counts_mt; normalize_total(target_sum=1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; scale; pca; neighbors; leiden; umap; rank_genes_groups (wilcoxon) for markers. Plots: QC violin, HVG, UMAP colored by leiden, marker dotplot."}

blocks.text: Now let me load the data and run the standard workflow:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Load 10x matrix triplet
prefix = f'{DATA_DIR}/GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1'
adata = sc.read_10x_mtx(
    f'{DATA_DIR}',
    var_names='gene_symbols',
    cache=False,
    gex_only=True
)

# Since there are multiple samples, I need to be more specific
# Let me reload just the first sample
import scipy.io as io
import gzip

# Read matrix
matrix_file = f'{prefix}.matrix.mtx.gz'
barcodes_file = f'{prefix}.barcodes.tsv.gz'
features_file = f'{prefix}.features.tsv.gz'

with gzip.open(matrix_file, 'rt') as f:
    matrix = io.mmread(f).T.tocsr()

with gzip.open(barcodes_file, 'rt') as f:
    barcodes = [line.strip() for line in f]

with gzip.open(features_file, 'rt') as f:
    features = pd.read_csv(f, sep='\t', header=None)

# Create AnnData object
adata = sc.AnnData(X=matrix)
adata.obs_names = barcodes
adata.var_names = features[1].values  # gene symbols
adata.var['gene_id'] = features[0].values

print(f"Data shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"First 5 obs names: {adata.obs_names[:5].tolist()}")
print(f"First 5 var names: {adata.var_names[:5].tolist()}")
print(f"Data type: {adata.X.dtype}")
print(f"X is sparse: {hasattr(adata.X, 'tocsr')}")
